auditMeta.method: read-only browser_evaluate of document.head per page
knownLimitations: /sign-in and /sign-up redirect to /catalog (no standalone auth pages on this build; matches existing memory note)
Account pages not visited (would require auth; QA focus here is on public-indexable pages)

indexHtmlShell.note: `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">` is hardcoded in the SPA shell and appears on every page. On QA this is acceptable; in production this must be overridden per page or removed from index.html.

pages.label: Homepage
pages.title: Virto Commerce
pages.notes: Falls back to the static index.html title ('Virto Commerce'). usePageHead() is never called on this route. Zero OG and zero JSON-LD.

pages.label: Category (by-id → slug redirect)
pages.title: QA & Allbiz
pages.notes: og:url points at the ID form, not the canonical slug form that the browser actually displays — this duplicates URL identity. No BreadcrumbList.

pages.label: Category (slug)
pages.title: QA & Coffee and tea
pages.notes: No BreadcrumbList JSON-LD. No canonical. Slug URL and id URL both render same content without a canonical to unify them.

pages.label: Brands landing
pages.title: QA & Brands
pages.notes: Zero OG, zero JSON-LD. The page renders only `<title>` and the hardcoded noindex.

pages.label: Search results
pages.title: QA & Catalog
pages.h1: Your search for hoodie returned the following 4 results
pages.notes: Search noindex is correct industry practice (and matches the shell's global noindex). Title 'QA & Catalog' is generic — it should include the query or at least the result count. og:url correctly reflects the search query.

pages.label: PDP (simple)
pages.title: QA & UNTUCKit eGift Card
pages.notes: CAT-040 finding confirmed. og:image URL is double-encoded ('%25252C' = %2C encoded twice) — a bug on top of the SEO gap. No Product JSON-LD, no canonical, empty meta description and og:description. og:type should be 'product' for PDPs.

pages.label: PDP (configurable product)
pages.title: QA & AGENT-TEST-Config-Gift-Box-20260327
pages.notes: Same gaps as simple PDP plus no og:image.

pages.label: Cart
pages.title: QA & Cart
pages.notes: Expected noindex. All other SEO fields acceptably empty for a transactional page.

pages.label: Compare products
pages.title: QA & Compare products
pages.notes: Description is a literal placeholder 'Test compare products page description' — translation key leak or admin default left in place. Bug-worthy in isolation.

pages.label: Contacts
pages.title: QA & Contacts
pages.notes: Missing description and Organization JSON-LD (typical for a Contact Us page).

pages.label: Bulk order
pages.title: QA & Bulk order
pages.notes: Description is a weak 3-word stub. Utility page — noindex acceptable.

pages.label: Catalog (/catalog)
pages.title: QA & Catalog
pages.notes: Description literally 'catalog' — single lowercase word, not a real description. og:description is the same. This confirms the usePageHead().meta pipeline IS firing on this route but the data source is empty/placeholder.

pages.title: QA & 404 Page not found
pages.notes: Returns 200 (soft-404) — no HTTP 404. Soft-404 is itself an SEO bug separate from this one.

metaRobots.coverage: 100% — but all are hardcoded 'noindex' from index.html shell

infraFound.capabilities: Only sets <title> + <meta name="X" content="Y"> pairs. Does NOT set <link rel="canonical">, <meta property="og:*">, <link rel="alternate" hreflang>, or <script type="application/ld+json">.

shellIssue.impact: Every page is noindex because the shell hardcodes it and usePageHead only appends — it does not override existing robots meta. This would destroy SEO in production.
